title: Nobody likes a party crasher
gist: Halina Bennet writes that Trump and the MAGA movement have turned America's 250th anniversary into a partisan branding exercise, leaving her unable to celebrate a milestone she believes should embody the country's open, meritocratic founding bet. The paywall cuts the piece off after its opening argument.

insights.claim: Halina Bennet frames the U.S. semiquincentennial as the anniversary of a country that has functioned for 250 years as a bet that people who arrive with nothing can build something, a founding premise she says is being overshadowed by partisan branding.
insights.speaker: Halina Bennet
insights.so_what: Signals a live media narrative fight over how the 250th anniversary milestone is framed, relevant to any political or civic messaging tied to it through 2026.
insights.quote: has functioned for a quarter millennium as a bet that people who show up with nothing can build something.

insights.claim: Bennet contends the Trump administration and Trumpism have spent roughly the past year and a half converting the nonpartisan 250th-anniversary commemoration into a partisan branding exercise she calls 'inherently unpatriotic.'
insights.speaker: Halina Bennet
insights.so_what: Flags a specific politicization of the July 2026 semiquincentennial events that could affect corporate and civic sponsorship or messaging decisions tied to the anniversary.
insights.quote: Donald Trump and Trumpism have spent the past year and a half turning the anniversary into a branding exercise to further the inherently unpatriotic MAGA project.
insights.locator: Donald Trump and Trumpism have spent

insights.claim: Bennet argues the fact that a nonpartisan national anniversary has become something people feel they must actively track is itself evidence of how politicized the 250th commemoration has become.
insights.speaker: Halina Bennet
insights.so_what: A gauge for how deeply the anniversary has bled into partisan politics and news-cycle fatigue.
insights.quote: a nonpartisan anniversary should not be something to keep an eye on
insights.locator: You'd be forgiven for not following
